author: ben_a_adams
content: Putting up the cost of an ETH transfer in EIP-2780 for the ETH transfer log https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11493

author: sky.cactus
content: Changing the 21,000 to something else is going to break a *lot* of tooling/apps built on top of Ethereum.  I really do feel like we must be willing to change the 21,000 cost over time, but at the same time it is probably the single change that will have the biggest impact downstream.  And I'm guessing we will see almost none of that impact until production, when every exchange's deposits and withdraws break and every app that doesn't test on public testnets switches over.

author: ben_a_adams
content: Is mostly reducing the cost so they shouldn't break from quoting too high a gaslimit?

author: sky.cactus
content: 21,000 => 31,756 is the one I'm worried about.

author: ben_a_adams
content: Might be wilder from the other repricing of state increase gas changes

author: sky.cactus
content: I think most tooling in the wild is better designed to deal with changing gas costs in most places, *except* the base 21,000.  In my experience, that is a hard coded constant in a lot of places.

author: ben_a_adams
content: Is a placeholder price currently as this one isn't determining what a new account create costs

author: sky.cactus
content: Yeah, it is just the change of 21,000 to anything >21,000 that I worry is a problem.  Even 21,001 I suspect would break a huge amount of downstream tooling.

author: ben_a_adams
content: Actual price for `GAS_NEW_ACCOUNT` would come from this one  https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-8037

author: sky.cactus
content: Yeah.  I replied to your message only because that is what caused me to become aware of the proposed increase in base transaction gas costs.

author: mariusvanderwijden
content: The problem is we can not scale ethereum if we don't increase the cost for account creation

author: mariusvanderwijden
content: I agree that for normal transfers we need to stay under 21k

author: ben_a_adams
content: Is a loophole atm that you can create for free at tx level (though overcharged for non-account creating tx); but then charged for creation only on CALL

author: bridge-bot
content: <vb271828> yeah that's one major loophole right now (re @eth1x_bridge_bot: <ben_a_adams> Is a loophole atm that you can create for free at tx level (though overcharged for non-account creating tx); but then charged for creation only on CALL)

author: sky.cactus
content: I agree that we should figure out a way to make 21,000 not fixed for Ethereum sustainability reasons.  "Scaling" is probably the worst approach to take with me in convincing me that it is a good idea though.  😬  Focus on things like "account abstraction" instead.  😉

author: bridge-bot
content: <vb271828> the goal is that we want resources to be priced relative to their cost, so if we can handle 1000 state creations per block and 100000 ecrecovers, then a state creation should cost 100x what an ecrecover costs

author: sky.cactus
content: I just want to make sure that there is a *huge* amount of thought and effort put into figuring out how to minimize the number of apps on Ethereum that break, especially immutable apps with immutable UIs.

author: bridge-bot
content: <vb271828> (insert big asterisks because marginal cost, economics is complex, etc, but to a first approximation that's true)

author: sky.cactus
content: I fully agree that this should be our targeting strategy.  My concern is only around the impact this change will have *outside* core dev work.  I can imagine this change going all the way through to a fork with almost no trouble and very easy implementation, then on fork day we hear screams from every corner of the internet that everything broke.

author: bridge-bot
content: <vb271828> yeah I agree it's one of the riskiest things in the whole transition

author: bridge-bot
content: <vb271828> I had suggested that we keep the 21k as is, and instead reduce the gas cost of everything else, make EVM ops cost 1 gas 🤣

author: bridge-bot
content: <vb271828> though apparently dev tooling people hate that

author: ben_a_adams
content: Can always warm/create the account by sending it some ETH (even 1wei) in a different tx; and then it will be on lower fee rate

author: bridge-bot
content: <vb271828> and also, you can only go down ~3x (to 1 gas)

author: bridge-bot
content: <vb271828> realistically the ratio between exec and creates ultimately needs to change by like 10-30x

author: sky.cactus
content: The one place where we definitely should have used bigger numbers in the EVM is the place we chose to use very small numbers.  😆

author: sky.cactus
content: If only gas costs were 100x bigger across the board, we would have a lot more breathing room at the bottom.

author: bridge-bot
content: <vb271828> Do we have anything like a list of dapps that hardcode 21k gas for anything?

author: bridge-bot
content: <vb271828> intuitively it would be wallets that do that, dapps generally do things other than simple send

author: sky.cactus
content: And exchanges, which I know from experience have a history of doing ETH sends with a fixed 21,000 gas cost (because they sometimes break when withdrawing to a contract account).

author: sky.cactus
content: One would hope that by now they are better (been a while since I have tried), but I'm not particularly confident that is the case.

author: bridge-bot
content: <vb271828> but yeah doing all this safely is going to be super important

author: mariusvanderwijden
content: My hardware wallet from 5 years ago hardcoded a limit of 100.000

author: mariusvanderwijden
content: You can always just create the account from a different wallet if your wallet has the limit hardcoded

author: funnygiulio
content: I think wallets will upgrade as soon as their user will complain at the speed of light. We are going to be fine. I am not sure what the risk is aside from some dev time
